skill.name: plugin-settings
skill.description: Guides plugin authors in storing per-project settings in .claude/*.local.md files with YAML frontmatter and markdown notes.
skill.summary: Create and parse local plugin settings files for Claude Code workflows.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many shell and filesystem patterns, plus high weak-crypto hits. Review found the weak-crypto alerts are false positives from markdown frontmatter separators, while the real risk is moderate shell-based parsing and prompt replay from local settings files. No malicious exfiltration, network access, or prompt-injection control text was found.

medium_findings.title: Local Settings Body Can Be Reused As Prompt Text
medium_findings.description: The skill documents hooks that extract markdown body content from a local settings file and return it as prompt text. This is useful for workflow loops, but untrusted edits to the settings file could influence model behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced examples explicitly read markdown body text and feed it back through hook output. The risk depends on who can modify the local settings file, so this is moderate rather than high.

medium_findings.title: Shell-Based Parsing Uses User-Controlled Field Names And Values
medium_findings.description: The utility scripts and examples use grep, sed, awk, and heredocs to parse or create settings files. This is legitimate local tooling, but careless reuse with untrusted field names or free text can cause parsing errors, regex surprises, or malformed settings files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell pipelines are real and can process user-provided settings data, but the skill also includes validation guidance. I did not find evidence of deliberate command injection or exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Settings-Controlled Tmux Target In Documentation Examples
medium_findings.description: The documentation shows a coordinator session read from settings and passed to tmux send-keys. If copied into a plugin without validation, a tampered settings file could redirect local notifications to an unintended tmux session.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command target is settings-derived, which creates a plausible misuse path. The shown payload is only a notification string, so the impact appears limited.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak-cryptography detections correspond to YAML frontmatter delimiters and markdown examples, not hash functions, ciphers, or cryptographic decisions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines are markdown or YAML frontmatter examples. No cryptographic primitive is present at these locations.

low_findings.title: Sensitive And System Path Alerts Are Defensive Checks
low_findings.description: The example hook checks for .env, secret, /etc, and /sys paths to deny risky file operations. These findings indicate defensive filtering rather than secret collection or system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code emits denial messages when sensitive or system paths are detected. I did not find reads from those sensitive files in the inspected context.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Shell Utilities In Scripts And Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on bash, sed, grep, awk, jq, yq, tmux, mv, and related shell tools. These are expected for this skill, but generated plugins should quote variables and validate settings before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell utilities are directly present and intentionally documented. The pattern is legitimate but security-sensitive when copied into hooks.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Hidden Settings File Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill standardizes reads and writes under .claude/*.local.md. This local filesystem access is central to the skill and should remain user-local and gitignored.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The local settings-file pattern is repeatedly documented and implemented. The files are intended to be local, but they may contain sensitive project preferences.

content.value_statement: Plugin authors need a simple way to store per-project preferences without committing user state. This skill documents a .claude/*.local.md pattern for readable settings, hook behavior, and validation.
actual_capabilities: Shows YAML frontmatter templates for plugin configuration and state.
Demonstrates bash parsing of settings fields and markdown bodies.
Includes example hook behavior controlled by local settings.
Provides scripts to parse and validate settings file structure.
Documents gitignore and restart guidance for local settings files.
limitations: Does not provide a full YAML parser for complex nested settings.
Requires careful validation before using settings in hooks or commands.
Settings changes may require restarting Claude Code for hook reloads.
Examples are templates and must be adapted to each plugin threat model.

use_cases.title: Add Project Settings To A Plugin
use_cases.description: Define a local settings file, document supported fields, and read those fields from plugin commands or hooks.

use_cases.title: Control Hook Behavior Per Project
use_cases.description: Use an enabled flag, validation level, or file limit to adjust hook behavior without changing plugin code.

use_cases.title: Store Temporary Agent State
use_cases.description: Track task identifiers, coordinator sessions, iteration counts, or workflow notes in a local markdown settings file.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Settings
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new plugin
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the plugin-settings pattern to design a basic local settings file for my plugin. Include enabled, mode, defaults, and gitignore guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Read Settings In A Hook
prompt_templates.prompt: Show how my Claude Code hook should read .claude/my-plugin.local.md, validate enabled and strict_mode, and fall back to safe defaults.

prompt_templates.title: Validate A Settings Schema
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this planned plugin settings schema for unsafe fields, missing validation, restart notes, and values that should not be stored locally.

prompt_templates.title: Design Stateful Workflow Control
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a local settings and state pattern for a multi-step agent workflow, including iteration limits, coordinator metadata, prompt safety, and cleanup behavior.

output_examples.input: A plugin author asks how to add project-specific validation settings.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a local settings-file design, field descriptions, default behavior, and notes about validation and restart requirements.

output_examples.input: A team wants a hook to run only when strict mode is enabled.
output_examples.output: The skill outlines a hook flow that checks for the local file, reads frontmatter, validates booleans, and exits safely when disabled.

output_examples.input: An automation engineer needs temporary state for a looping workflow.
output_examples.output: The skill describes a local markdown state file with counters, completion criteria, and cleanup rules.

best_practices: Keep local settings files gitignored and readable only by the user.
Validate every frontmatter value before it affects hooks, commands, or paths.
Provide safe defaults when the settings file is missing or incomplete.
anti_patterns: Do not store tokens, passwords, or shared secrets in local markdown settings files.
Do not feed settings body text back to the model without trust and scope checks.
Do not use settings-derived paths or command targets without validation.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps you add local, per-project configuration files for Claude Code plugins.

faq.question: Where are settings files stored?
faq.answer: The documented pattern uses .claude/plugin-name.local.md inside the project directory.

faq.question: Should these files be committed to git?
faq.answer: No. The skill recommends gitignoring .claude/*.local.md and .claude/*.local.json.

faq.question: Can the settings include markdown content?
faq.answer: Yes. The pattern supports YAML frontmatter for fields and markdown body text for notes or workflow context.

faq.question: Does it support complex YAML automatically?
faq.answer: No. The examples focus on simple fields and suggest yq for complex YAML structures.

faq.question: What is the main security concern?
faq.answer: Settings are local input. Validate values before using them in hooks, paths, commands, or prompts.
